# Donny — Backend Engineer

> Donny (donny eats tech) is a backend engineer specializing in distributed systems, databases, and infrastructure. Currently Senior Software Engineer at Clientell, where he owns the AI-agent execution path and built the Salesforce org-indexing platform. Based in Bengaluru, India. Writes long-form technical deep-dives on Substack.

## About

- **Role:** Senior Software Engineer at Clientell (Oct 2025 – present)
- **Focus:** Backend engineering — distributed systems, databases, infrastructure, observability
- **Location:** Bengaluru, India
- **Core stack:** Go, Python, PostgreSQL, Redis, FastAPI, GCP, AWS, Pub/Sub, OpenTelemetry
- **Interests:** Postgres internals, consistent hashing, Raft/consensus, systems programming

## Experience

- **Clientell — Senior Software Engineer (Oct 2025 – present):** Owns the AI-agent execution path end to end; built the Salesforce org-indexing platform as an orchestrator/worker system on Pub/Sub (~28k work items/run, autoscaling to 100 Cloud Run instances). Cut tool-call latency from ~4s to ~800ms. Clientell is an AI agent for Salesforce deployed across 500+ orgs including Notion, Rakuten, and PwC.
- **Fae — Lead Backend Engineer (Jan 2025 – Oct 2025):** Rewrote a legacy NestJS codebase into a two-service FastAPI monolith; implemented ABAC authorization with audit logging; halved payment execution latency from ~5s to ~2.5s.
- **Gida Technologies — Software Engineer (Oct 2022 – Nov 2023):** Built a daily ETL pipeline ingesting ~600k health-insurance records into BigQuery with PGP encryption for India's Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).

## Writing

- [Yes, you can store JSONs in Postgres. No, you shouldn't. Well, it depends](https://substack.com/@donnyeatstech/p-164607285): PostgreSQL JSON/JSONB storage mechanics, indexing tradeoffs, and query performance.
- [From the Birthday Paradox to Consistent Hashing](https://substack.com/@donnyeatstech/p-162607285): A conceptual bridge from probability to load distribution in distributed systems.

## Projects

- [redis-go](https://github.com/donnyeatstech/redis-go): Redis-compatible server in Go — RESP2 protocol, TTL expiry, SQLite-backed durability.
- [mit-6.5840](https://github.com/donnyeatstech/mit-6.5840): MIT's distributed systems course — Raft, MapReduce, and distributed key-value stores.
